title: B12 deficiency endocrine review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.
examination.rationale: The exact recognition section supports onset, interval change, functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient concerns for a B12-deficiency review without confirming deficiency or cause.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports variable and nonspecific symptom context, neurological, cognitive, visual, fatigue, anaemia, diet, medicine, surgery and other risk-factor documentation.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.6 and Boxes 1–2 — recognition, symptoms and risk factors

investigations.text: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.
investigations.rationale: The exact test and result-context section supports focused assessment and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation, including supplement and assay context, without autonomously interpreting the result.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports recording the type of vitamin B12 test, pregnancy or nitrous-oxide context, current supplements, laboratory reference context, indeterminate results and clinician-led further assessment without autonomously interpreting a result.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.16 — tests, result context and interpretation factors

medication.text: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.
medication.rationale: The exact follow-up section supports recording worsening or new symptoms, clinician concern, a clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net and arranged follow-up; medicine route and dose remain unsupported.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports symptom response, worsening or new symptoms, adherence context and clinician-agreed reassessment or follow-up. Medicine route, dose and frequency are excluded from mappings.

source.title: Vitamin B12 deficiency in over 16s: diagnosis and management

medication.text: Supports clinician-documented autoimmune-gastritis, coeliac, medicine, diet, surgery and other cause-review context without asserting a cause.
medication.rationale: Cause-identification recommendations were reviewed, but no cause is inferred or inserted.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-documented autoimmune-gastritis, coeliac, medicine, diet, surgery and other cause-review context without asserting a cause.

source_limitations: Specific symptoms, diet, surgery, medicines, nitrous oxide, blood count, B12 assay, MMA, homocysteine, antibodies, cause, diagnosis, treatment, dose and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
